commands.repair_reason: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because no concrete patch defect was found; the remaining action is maintainer review and CI gating.; Cleared: The diff is limited to Gateway/session/UI TypeScript source and tests and does not add dependencies, workflows, package metadata, secrets handling, or code-execution surfaces. commands.review_summary:
The PR projects provenance-backed `sessions_send` inter-session user turns into assistant-side forwarded WebChat display messages, strips the inter-session envelope for display, and lets assistant groups render `senderLabel` attribution.

PR surface: Source +108, Tests +326. Total +434 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main persists `sessions_send` turns as `role: "user"` with inter-session provenance, lacks a `sessions_send` display projection, and WebChat currently groups/render labels from role-based user handling.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because no concrete patch defect was found; the remaining action is maintainer review and CI gating.

target.title: fix(webchat): show sessions_send handoffs as forwarded
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89161.

- Project `sessions_send` inter-session user turns as assistant-side WebChat display messages while preserving the persisted transcript `role: "user"` contract.
- Strip the generated inter-session prompt envelope from visible forwarded content.
- Let WebChat assistant groups use `senderLabel`, so forwarded messages render with source attribution such as `Forwarded from main` instead of appearing as `You`.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/sessions/input-provenance.test.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:fix src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/sessions/input-provenance.ts src/sessions/input-provenance.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- Browser WebChat proof with Control UI E2E mock Gateway, using production `buildSessionHistorySnapshot` projection output as the browser `chat.startup` history source.

Known proof gap:

- `pnpm check:test-types` timed out locally before completion; split relevant core/UI lanes passed and CI `check-test-types` passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` also timed out locally. This patch does not touch `extensions/`.
- The browser proof uses deterministic mocked Gateway transport with production projection output, not a live provider-backed two-agent run.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions_send` inter-session messages no longer render as right-side user-authored WebChat bubbles; display projection marks them as assistant-side forwarded messages with sender attribution.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout, running OpenClaw production WebChat display projection code through `buildSessionHistorySnapshot`, then rendering the resulting `chat.startup` history in a real Chromium Control UI/WebChat browser session with the existing mocked Gateway WebSocket harness.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a Playwright/Chromium browser proof via `node --import tsx`. The script feeds a transcript-shaped raw `role: "user"` message with `provenance.kind: "inter_session"` and `sourceTool: "sessions_send"` into `buildSessionHistorySnapshot`, passes `snapshot.history.messages` to `installMockGateway`, opens `/chat`, waits for the forwarded text and attribution, captures a screenshot/video, and reads the rendered DOM.

Evidence after fix:

```
{
  "inputRole": "user",
  "inputSourceTool": "sessions_send",
  "projectedRole": "assistant",
  "projectedSenderLabel": "Forwarded from main",
  "projectedText": "forwarded report from Dollar for browser proof",
  "projectedHasInterSessionEnvelope": false,
  "browserForwardedGroupClass": "chat-group assistant",
  "browserForwardedSender": "Forwarded from main",
  "browserForwardedText": "forwarded report from Dollar for browser proof Forwarded from main Jun 2, 2026, 2:00 AM",
  "browserHasForwardedText": true,
  "browserHasForwardedLabel": true,
  "browserHasInterSessionEnvelope": false,
  "screenshotPath": ".artifacts/control-ui-e2e/sessions-send-forwarded/webchat-sessions-send-forwarded.png",
  "videoPath": ".artifacts/control-ui-e2e/sessions-send-forwarded/page@4dcd3d9dbabdc50973b5b3ba266801e3.webm"
}
```

Observed result after fix: The production WebChat history projection preserves the underlying inter-session provenance, changes the display role to `assistant`, adds `Forwarded from main`, removes the generated `[Inter-session message]` envelope from visible text, and the real browser DOM renders the handoff as `.chat-group assistant` with the forwarded sender label.

What was not tested: Live provider-backed two-agent WebChat run; full local combined `check:test-types`; extensions test type lane.

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

target.title: feat: GitHub App source-controlled skill sync
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a GitHub App install/setup flow for publishers with HMAC-signed setup state, single-use state consumption, webhook signature verification, and installation webhook claims for org-account handshakes.
- Add repository links, source-link mappings, sync jobs, webhook delivery tracking, and source-managed publish enforcement so only linked skills sync while manual org skills remain untouched.
- Add repository sync from pinned GitHub archive commits, push-job coalescing/serialization, repository removal/rename handling, and source provenance checks.
- Enforce version-level scan gates for explicit historical skill artifact access so a superseded malicious or pending ClawScan/VT version cannot be downloaded or served just because the current latest version is active.
- Document the source-management invariants, version security boundary, and GitHub App operational setup in `specs/github-app-skill-sync.md`.

## GitHub App setup notes

Required env:

- `GITHUB_APP_ID`
- `GITHUB_APP_SLUG`
- `GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY`
- `GITHUB_APP_WEBHOOK_SECRET`
- optional `GITHUB_APP_STATE_SECRET`

App permissions/events:

- Contents: read-only
- Metadata: read-only
- Webhook URL: `/api/webhooks/github-app`
- Events: `push`, `installation`, `installation_repositories`, `repository`

Repository links are disabled by default after installation until an org admin enables sync. Org install completion requires a signed GitHub installation webhook claim matching the current user's GitHub account; if the redirect beats the webhook, retry completion after the webhook arrives.

## Security boundary note

GitHub sync publishes through the normal skill publish pipeline and queues the normal scanners for each new `skillVersions` row. Scanner completion only promotes verdicts to the skill-level moderation row when the scanned version is still latest, which prevents stale async results from overwriting the current latest verdict.

Because historical versions remain addressable by explicit `version` or `tag`, artifact-serving endpoints now also enforce the requested version's own ClawScan/VT fields. Download, card, raw file, and version-detail endpoints block malicious or explicitly pending historical versions; static-scan-only findings remain advisory when ClawScan/VT do not block the version; scan and verification endpoints remain readable so callers can inspect the blocked version's security context.

## Tests run so far

- `bunx vitest run convex/lib/githubAppSync.test.ts`
- `bunx vitest run convex/downloads.test.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts`
- `bun run format:check -- convex/lib/skillFileAccess.ts convex/downloads.ts convex/httpApiV1/skillsV1.ts convex/httpApiV1/packagesV1.ts convex/downloads.test.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts specs/github-app-skill-sync.md`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`
- `git diff --check`

## Still running / follow-up on this PR

- Re-run autoreview after the latest accepted fixes.
- Run full pre-handoff gates: `bun run ci:static`, `bun run ci:unit`, `bun run ci:types-build`.
- `bunx convex codegen` was blocked locally by missing Convex deployment/login config; generated API typing was updated manually and verified by TypeScript/build checks.
target.author: romneyda
